length_balance.note: Each MCQ's options are matched in word count; correct answer carries the same 'value + brief reason' shape as its distractors.

objective_coverage: Read a confusion matrix and name each of its four cells (TP, FP, FN, TN)
Define accuracy and write its formula in terms of the four cells
Compute accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 by hand from a given confusion matrix
Build a confusion matrix yourself by tallying a small table of predictions against the truth
Explain why accuracy alone can mislead when one class is rare

questions.objective_ref: Read a confusion matrix and name each of its four cells: TP, FP, FN, TN
Define accuracy and write its formula in terms of the four cells
Compute precision by hand from a given confusion matrix (read the column, not the row)
Compute recall by hand from a given confusion matrix (read the row, not the column)
Build a confusion matrix by tallying predictions against the truth and sanity-check the total
Compute precision by hand from a given small confusion matrix
Explain why accuracy alone can mislead when one class is rare (which metric reveals the missed positives and why)
accepted_answers: recall = TP/(TP+FN) = 0 while accuracy stays high because true negatives dominate a rare-positive total
